mission.title: Install and Configure Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)
mission.description: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment, management, and operations of Kubernetes clusters in the Azure cloud. It is ideal for users looking to leverage Kubernetes without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on Azure's ecosystem and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install Azure CLI
steps.description:
The Azure CLI is required to interact with Azure resources and manage your AKS cluster.

```bash

curl -sL https://aka.ms/InstallAzureCLIDeb | sudo bash

```

> This command installs the Azure CLI on Debian-based systems.

steps.title: Create AKS Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new AKS cluster with the specified Kubernetes version, node count, and region.

```bash

az aks create --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --node-count 3 --kubernetes-version 1.31 --location eastus

```

> Replace 'myResourceGroup' and 'myAKSCluster' with your desired names.
commands: az aks create --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --node-count 3 --kubernetes-version 1.31 --location eastus

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig file to connect to the newly created AKS cluster.

```bash

az aks get-credentials --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster

```

> This command merges the AKS cluster credentials into your kubeconfig.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your AKS cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for your AKS cluster to ensure optimal performance.

```bash

az aks update --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --enable-cluster-autoscaler --min-count 1 --max-count 5

az monitor autoscale create --resource-group myResourceGroup --resource myAKSCluster --resource-type Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters --name myAutoscale --min-count 1 --max-count 5 --count 3

```

> This enables cluster autoscaler with specified min and max node counts.
> This sets up monitoring for the cluster.
commands: az monitor autoscale create --resource-group myResourceGroup --resource myAKSCluster --resource-type Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters --name myAutoscale --min-count 1 --max-count 5 --count 3

uninstall.title: Delete AKS Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the AKS cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

az aks delete --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --yes --no-wait

```

> This command will delete the cluster without waiting for completion.

uninstall.title: Clean Up Resource Group
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the resource group and all resources within it, which may include orphaned resources.

```bash

az group delete --name myResourceGroup --yes --no-wait

```

> This will remove the entire resource group and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade AKS Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your workloads and configurations. This command upgrades the AKS cluster to the specified version.

```bash

az aks upgrade --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --kubernetes-version 1.31

```

> This command upgrades the AKS cluster to version 1.31.

upgrade.title: Verify Upgrade
upgrade.description:
After the upgrade, verify that the nodes are running the new version.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check that the nodes reflect the upgraded version.

upgrade.title: Post-Upgrade Configuration
upgrade.description:
Reapply any configurations that may have been reset during the upgrade.

```bash

az monitor autoscale update --resource-group myResourceGroup --resource myAKSCluster --resource-type Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters --name myAutoscale --count 3

```

> Ensure autoscaling settings are still in place.
commands: az monitor autoscale update --resource-group myResourceGroup --resource myAKSCluster --resource-type Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters --name myAutoscale --count 3

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create AKS cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient permissions or quota limits in your Azure subscription.

**Fix:**
Check your Azure subscription permissions and ensure you have enough quota for the requested resources.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubeconfig context not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This happens when the kubeconfig file does not contain the context for the AKS cluster.

**Fix:**
Run 'az aks get-credentials --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster' to update your kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Nodes not in 'Ready' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This could be due to insufficient resources or misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the node status with 'kubectl describe nodes' and ensure there are enough resources allocated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster autoscaler not working'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the cluster autoscaler is not configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Verify the autoscaler settings with 'az aks show --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --query 'agentPoolProfiles[].enableAutoScaling' -o table'.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new Azure regions and improved integration with Azure Monitor.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of certain legacy APIs for cluster management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure to review the new API changes and update any custom scripts accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' with all nodes in 'Ready' state, indicating that your AKS cluster is operational.
codeSnippets: az aks create --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --node-count 3 --kubernetes-version 1.31 --location eastus

az monitor autoscale create --resource-group myResourceGroup --resource myAKSCluster --resource-type Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters --name myAutoscale --min-count 1 --max-count 5 --count 3

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Azure CLI and kubectl installed and configured.
